ingredients.name: NaCl
ingredients.rationale: 25% NaCl (~4.3 M) — extreme halophile; cytoplasmic K+ concentration matched to external Na+.

ingredients.name: MgSO4·7H2O
ingredients.rationale: Elevated MgSO4 (~80 mM) typical of haloarchaeal media.

ingredients.name: KCl
ingredients.rationale: Potassium baseline (~25 mM) for halophile osmotic balance.

ingredients.name: Peptone
ingredients.rationale: Amino-acid carbon source for halobacterial growth.

ingredients.name: Phosphate buffer (KH2PO4 + K2HPO4)
ingredients.rationale: 30 mM phosphate buffer adjusted to pH 7.0

ingredients.name: SL-10 trace metal solution
ingredients.rationale: SL-10 (Tschech & Pfennig 1984): Fe, Zn, Mn, B, Co, Cu, Ni, Mo at standard concentrations. 1 mL/L final.

ingredients.name: Wolin's vitamin solution
ingredients.rationale: Wolin's vitamins (Wolin et al. 1963): biotin, folic acid, pyridoxine, thiamine, riboflavin, nicotinic acid, pantothenate, B12, p-aminobenzoic acid, lipoic acid. 1 mL/L final.

gas_phase.rationale: Aerobic; halobacteria are obligate aerobes despite having rhodopsin.

conditions.rationale: Temperature 30.0°C, pH 7.0 (default — no GenomeSPOT/TEMPURA data); shaking 150 rpm; ambient light for rhodopsin

thermodynamic_checks.primary_reaction: Light + H+ → H+ pumped (rhodopsin proton pumping; supplementary energy)

composition_rationale: Extreme halophile with bacteriorhodopsin. Aerobic heterotroph using amino acids; rhodopsin provides supplementary light-driven energy. High NaCl (~25%) is essential.
